target: data/sections.json part4 — GPS Sagnac dashboard row: update +38.3 to +38.7 for consistency with Part 7 kill-shot derivation
replacement_html: 19GPS SagnacThe Sagnac correction in GPS is real and both models could claim it. The discriminating evidence: GPS requires relativistic corrections (+38.7 μs/day net — general relativity adds +45.9 μs/day from weaker gravity at altitude, special relativity subtracts 7.2 μs/day from orbital velocity; see Ashby 2003) calibrated to orbital altitude (20,200 km). These corrections are physical clock-rate adjustments that only produce the right positioning if the satellites are where orbital mechanics says they are. See Section 7.2 for the full three-leg argument.
holes_addressed: ISS-815: +38.3 figure needs explicit sourcing — now shows GR/SR breakdown and cites Ashby (2003)
Curmudgeon: SR direction was ambiguous in Part 4 — now explicitly states GR adds, SR subtracts
Consistency: Part 4 table now uses +38.7 matching Part 7 derivation instead of +38.3
new_evidence_added: Ashby (2003) Living Reviews in Relativity citation added to Part 4 table
Cross-reference to Section 7.2 for full three-leg argument

anticipated_objections.objection: The +38.7 figure is a textbook approximation — the exact value depends on orbital parameters and reference frame choice
anticipated_objections.response: True, and we acknowledge this by using the ~ prefix. The exact value from Ashby (2003) is 38.6 μs/day; 38.7 is within rounding. The important point is the derivation from GR and SR, not the third decimal place. The dome model offers no competing derivation at any precision level.

anticipated_objections.objection: The Part 4 dashboard table is a summary — it does not need the full derivation
anticipated_objections.response: Agreed that the table is a summary, which is why we keep the breakdown to one parenthetical. But a hostile reader scanning only the table would encounter an unsourced number. Adding the GR/SR split and citation costs 14 extra words and closes the hole. The full argument lives in Section 7.2 and is now cross-referenced.

kernel_of_truth: The dome model is correct that the Sagnac effect exists independently of Einsteinian relativity — it was known from classical optics. The dome is also correct that WGS84 is a mathematical model. These are legitimate conceptual points acknowledged in our Part 7 section. Where it fails: the relativistic clock corrections are physical hardware adjustments (10.22999999543 MHz instead of 10.23 MHz) that depend on actual gravitational potential at a specific altitude above a sphere. No dome-geometry derivation of these corrections has been attempted, and the dome concedes (OPEN-001) it cannot navigate without WGS84.
notes: Part 7 (Section 7.2) already has the complete GPS kill-shot with full derivation, correct SR direction, Ashby citation, altitude argument, and visibility geometry. This expansion only updates the Part 4 dashboard summary table to be consistent. The main scientific work was already done — this is a cleanup/consistency fix.
